summary: The AIM Problem Lists dataset contains 101 open problems across 80+ mathematics topics, with the visible issue tracking showing predominantly resolved proof work (each listed problem has 0 open, 1 resolved issue). Activity is concentrated in additive combinatorics (aim_addcombapp series) and hypergraph Turán problems, all of which appear to have completed proofs. The proof status section shows a uniform distribution of 100 issues across questions q1-q10, suggesting a systematic, evenly-tracked verification workflow rather than uneven hotspots.
problems: Nearly all listed problems carry the generic placeholder title 'Proof of <id>: (untitled)', indicating missing or incomplete metadata for proof submissions
Uniform 100-issue counts across q1 through q10 are suspiciously regular, possibly indicating an automated or templated tracking artifact rather than genuine per-question activity
Only 7 of 101 problems have visible issue-level detail; the remaining ~94 problems lack reported activity in this context, creating a visibility gap
No problems currently show 'open' issues in the listed set, making it hard to identify genuinely active research fronts versus closed/archived work
The mismatch between 101 total problems and the q1-q10 status buckets (1000 issue-slots) is unexplained and may indicate duplicated or cross-tabulated counts
highlights: The additive combinatorics cluster (aim_addcombapp_0001-002) shows strong completion, with all six tracked problems fully resolved
aim_addcombapp_0002 ('3APs in sets of small growth') has a properly titled, resolved proof — a model example of well-documented progress
The hypergraph Turán problem (aim_hypergraphturan_001) is resolved, indicating traction in extremal combinatorics
100% resolution rate among all problems with visible issue tracking suggests an effective verification pipeline

suggested_todos.title: Backfill missing titles for all '(untitled)' proof entries to enable meaningful tracking
Surface issue-level data for the ~94 problems not shown in this context to find genuinely open research fronts
Audit the q1-q10 uniform 100-issue counts to confirm they reflect real activity versus a templating artifact
Document and verify the resolved additive combinatorics proofs while context is fresh, prioritizing aim_addcombapp_0002
Reconcile the 101 problems vs. 1000 q1-q10 issue-slots discrepancy in the dataset schema
Identify near-complete proofs (single open issue, partial proof) as candidates for fast resolution next week
